title: MDX Body Editor — Component Manifest
description: Defines JSX component and YAML frontmatter field definitions for the directus-extension-mdx-body-editor. Host this file at a URL your Directus instance can fetch and paste the URL into the field options.

ManifestObject.description: Preferred format. Supports both component and frontmatter validation.

components.description: JSX component definitions. Drives autocomplete, unknown-component warnings, and prop validation.

frontmatter.description: YAML frontmatter field definitions. Drives unknown-field warnings, required-field checks, and enum validation.

LegacyComponentArray.description: Legacy format: array of component entries with no frontmatter validation. Equivalent to { components: [...], frontmatter: [] }.

ComponentEntry.description: A single JSX component available in the MDX body.

name.description: Component name as used in JSX. Must start with an uppercase letter.

description.description: Short description shown in the autocomplete tooltip.

PropEntry.description: A single JSX prop on a component.

name.description: Prop name as written in JSX.

type.description: Type annotation. Use a union of quoted string literals for enum validation and autocomplete (e.g. '"info" | "warning"'). Use 'string', 'number', or 'boolean' for scalar types (no value suggestions).

required.description: If true, the linter warns when the prop is absent on an opening tag.

description.description: Human-readable description shown in the autocomplete detail panel.

FrontmatterField.description: A single YAML field expected in the frontmatter block.

name.description: Field key as it appears in the YAML frontmatter.

type.description: Type annotation. 'string', 'number', 'boolean', 'string[]' perform presence-only checks. A union of quoted string literals (e.g. '"draft" | "published"') also validates the scalar value.

required.description: If true, the linter warns when the field is absent from the frontmatter block.

description.description: Human-readable description (not currently shown in the editor UI, included for documentation purposes).
